expiryConditions: The exact @openai/codex-sdk, @openai/codex CLI or Windows platform-package version or integrity changes.
The resolved dependency tree, install-script inventory or official registry provenance changes.
The Electron, embedded Node, Forge, Vite, ASAR, externalization or package allowlist configuration changes in a way that can affect this boundary.
The utility protocol, SDK-only import guard, working-directory policy, environment filter, auth classification, structured-output validator or cleanup supervisor changes.
The SDK executable layout, codexPathOverride behavior, SDK-owned CLI/JSONL mechanism or ChatGPT-managed authentication mechanism changes.
The Windows target operating system, architecture, packaging format or release runtime differs from the verified Windows x64 package.
A required recertification cannot obtain a fresh authenticated real SDK success or returns a different auth, schema, cancellation, cleanup or packaged-runtime result.
A renderer, preload or shared boundary begins exposing SDK, filesystem, shell, child-process, environment, token, secret or secure-storage capability.
limitations: This is a conditional Go for Windows-only implementation feasibility, not a full Go.
macOS packaged runtime evidence is deferred-by-user and is required before any full or cross-platform Go claim.
Expired-session behavior and the natural WriteStorm login experience remain unverified.
This decision does not establish release readiness and does not authorize Task 13.2.
Direct codex exec, app-server, GUI automation, API Key, local-model and alternate-provider fallback remain forbidden.
